title: RPM packages for CentOS 8 Stream
body:
/kind question

**Description**

I apology in advance if this is the wrong place to ask.
I am aware of the work done by @lsm5 in https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman4/ (thanks again for that!) but that repository only targets CentOS 9 Stream & Fedora 35.
Are there any plans to make podman 4 available for CentOS 8 Stream in an official repository? (or close to an official one like the repository above)

PS : please note that this specifically calls for RPM packages for C8 Stream and not CentOS Linux 8 since the latter has reach EOL status since Dec. 31st, 2021.

Best regards,
François

comments.body:
@fpoirotte: The label(s) `kind/question` cannot be applied, because the repository doesn't have them.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13350):

>/kind question
>
>**Description**
>
>I apology in advance if this is the wrong place to ask.
>I am aware of the work done by @lsm5 in https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman4/ (thanks again for that!) but that repository only targets CentOS 9 Stream & Fedora 35.
>Are there any plans to make podman 4 available for CentOS 8 Stream in an official repository? (or close to an official one like the repository above)
>
>PS : please note that this specifically calls for RPM packages for C8 Stream and not CentOS Linux 8 since the latter has reach EOL status since Dec. 31st, 2021.
>
>Best regards,
>François


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
http://mirror.centos.org/centos/8-stream/AppStream/x86_64/os/Packages/

> http://mirror.centos.org/centos/8-stream/AppStream/x86_64/os/Packages/

Yeah... except I'm not really sure what version this actually is because the package was built in October 2021, but podman 4 wasn't actually released until this month!
So it seems a bit fishy even though it's part of the official repositories.
Podman v4.0 is scheduled for RHEL 8.6.0, so I presume it should be filtering into Cent Stream 8 in advance of that.

I think that `4.0.0-0.10.module_el8.6.0+944+d413f95e` was some kind of packaging accident, built from 4.0.0-dev.

https://koji.mbox.centos.org/koji/buildinfo?buildID=19463

<https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/800d594>

As opposed to https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/84c8870ac236578c41713113fc09a29a5f727bdd (`v4.0.0`), so wait for 4.0.1
@fpoirotte Let me try enabling the podman4 copr for c8s. If it proves too painful to maintain, I probably will disable it back. Let's see how it goes.

quick update, podman 4 is `built` for centos8 on the podman4 copr https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman4/ BUT when I try to enable it on my centos 8 container, it says `Error: This repository does not have any builds yet so you cannot enable it now.` Hopefully, the copr infra folks will be able to sort this out soon. I'll update here once that's done.

Instructions updated on the copr description: https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman4/ . It's a little different for centos 8 stream. I'll update the install docs on podman.io as well. I won't be able to account for mix-n-match between copr packages and the container-tools module available by default in centos 8. So, I would suggest disabling container-tools module before installing this.

@fpoirotte and others, please give it a try and let me know.

Getting this error with `podman run`, does go away with ` --security-opt=seccomp=unconfined`: `Error: loading seccomp profile (/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json) failed: seccomp not enabled in this build`

`podman logs` seem to be broken: `Error: Journald logging only enabled with systemd on linux: no support for this OS yet`

Also, it seems you need to install podman from container-tools first before using this repo.

One more weird thing: 
```
# podman -v
podman version 4.0.2
# podman network create -d ipvlan
Error: unsupported driver ipvlan: invalid argument
```
other 4.0 flags like --ip6 work though

> Getting this error with `podman run`, does go away with ` --security-opt=seccomp=unconfined`: `Error: loading seccomp profile (/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json) failed: seccomp not enabled in this build`

I do see that podman is built with seccomp in the rpm, but for whatever reason podman info shows `seccompEnabled: false`.  Both c9s and c8s builds use the same rpm source, but seccomp info looks different on both.

Centos 9 Stream
```
$ podman info | grep seccomp
 seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
      libseccomp: 2.5.2
```

CentOS 8 Stream
```
$ podman info | grep seccomp
    seccompEnabled: false
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
```

@mheon any idea what else would be missing ?


> 
> `podman logs` seem to be broken: `Error: Journald logging only enabled with systemd on linux: no support for this OS yet`

I have commented out `log_driver = journald` from containers.conf. If you update your containers-common package to `4:1-46` this should be resolved.

> 
> Also, it seems you need to install podman from container-tools first before using this repo.

I have added missing dependencies to the copr itself, so you shouldn't need to install podman or any other packages from container-tools before installing from the copr.

> 
> One more weird thing:
> 
> ```
> # podman -v
> podman version 4.0.2
> # podman network create -d ipvlan
> Error: unsupported driver ipvlan: invalid argument
> ```
> 
> other 4.0 flags like --ip6 work though

@Luap99 @mheon is the ipvlan network plugin expected on centos 8? I find it on fedora but not on centos 8 stream and 9 stream
ipvlan does not work with netavark, only cni supports ipvlan
Re: seccomp - either the default profile listed is missing, or Podman was built without libseccomp support (bad buildtags?)

> `podman logs` seem to be broken: `Error: Journald logging only enabled with systemd on linux: no support for this OS yet`

This sounds like podman was compiled without systemd build tag.

hmm, in the build logs I do see the buildtags being used : ` BUILDTAGS='seccomp exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper selinux systemd libsubid exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion` is present in https://download.copr.fedorainfracloud.org/results/rhcontainerbot/podman4/centos-stream-8-x86_64/03670783-podman/build.log.gz . I'll look further though.



> > `podman logs` seem to be broken: `Error: Journald logging only enabled with systemd on linux: no support for this OS yet`
> 
> This sounds like podman was compiled without systemd build tag.

@Luap99  I see that in rhel8, we have set log_driver to k8s-file https://git.centos.org/rpms/containers-common/blob/c8s-stream-rhel8/f/SOURCES/containers.conf#_156 . Should we prefer k8s-file in the centos 8 copr, or would journald be preferable assuming systemd build tag is doable.
Just do not explicitly set this field at all, the build-in default should be able to figure this out on its own

> Just do not explicitly set this field at all, the build-in default should be able to figure this out on its own

great, the latest build on the copr leaves it commented.
Is this still an issue?

The buildtags stuff is still an issue, but I don't want to spend any more time on it since podman4 will land in centos 8 stream soon. I'm also not in the mood to update the podman installation docs to mention this copr as I'm not sure if I'll keep supporting centos 8 stream on that copr for long.

Hope people don't mind running it with seccomp unconfined.

Closing..

I installed the COPR build on centos 8 stream, looking for a fix for https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14054 - even `podman run alpine` now fails with `Error: loading seccomp profile (/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json) failed: seccomp not enabled in this build`. Maybe not worth publishing them after all.
podman 4 is now on centos 8 stream's default repos. So I'm getting rid of them from the copr.
